pages.full_text:
for flying kites. There's a big community room with wood-burning stove for drying out & keeping warm if the weather's bad.
	Simon + Eve + Tinder went to __Frampton on Severn__ to walk along village, fields, canal. Tinder v. good. Then on to lunch in a field. Then on to home.

Sunday April 28th
 We shd have left at 8.30., but we left 5 min late because TT, parked in front of van, was flat, so Simon had to recharge it. We are booked into a 10.30. tour of Dern Gate, Mackintosh's house in Northampton. We had wanted to camp this w/end, but it is too cold.
	We reached Derngate at 10 & went on a guided tour. The house was built 1816 & modernised for Bassett-Lowke by Mackintosh in 1916. The house cost ~~£820~~ £220 & the renovations cost £800 (workman's weekly wage £2). BL was colourblind & only saw yellow & black.
pages.notes: Page appears to be a continuation from a previous page (text starts mid-sentence with 'for flying kites'). No handwritten page number visible in top-left. 'Dern Gate' and 'Derngate' both appear — the diarist uses both forms; Derngate is 78 Derngate, Northampton, the Mackintosh-designed house. 'BL' refers to Bassett-Lowke. Sunday April 28th: in volume B (2003–2019), April 28 fell on a Sunday in 2013 and 2019. The '78 Derngate' caption on the right page corroborates this visit. Year cannot be confirmed from the page text alone. The strikethrough on '£820' is in the original — diarist corrected to £220. 'TT' likely refers to a car/vehicle battery tender.

pages.full_text:
78 Derngate. Hall/lounge screen on side of stair

	On the way home we stopped with Judith Kirby for lunch at Bierton, then had tea on the roadside next to a field of sheep near Bicester.
photo_captions: 78 Derngate. Hall/lounge screen on side of stair
pages.notes: Page contains a glued-in postcard/printed photograph of a Charles Rennie Mackintosh stained glass hall/lounge screen with printed caption 'CHARLES RENNIE MACKINTOSH'. Below the photo is a handwritten caption '78 Derngate. Hall/lounge screen on side of stair', then narrative text. Handwritten page number '19' visible in top-right corner. 'Bierton' is a village near Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire. 'Bicester' is in Oxfordshire — consistent with a route home from Northampton heading south.
